title: Case 89430: College Decision 058/26/COL - Norway - Methodology for sharing Firmness and Remuneration Costs (FRC)
text:
Case No: 89430
Document No: 1584796
Decision No: 058/26/COL
EFTA SURVEILLANCE AUTHORITY DECISION
of 18 March 2026
on the methodology for sharing Firmness and Remuneration Costs (FRC) of long-
term transmission rights for Norway
THE EFTA SURVEILLANCE AUTHORITY,
Having regard to:
the Agreement on the European Economic Area1 (‘the EEA Agreement’), in particular to
Article 24 and Annex IV thereof,
the Act referred to at point 47 of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement:
Regulation (EC) No 713/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13
July 2009 establishing an Agency for the Cooperation of Energy Regulators (‘the
ACER Regulation’),2 and in particular Article 8 thereof, as adapted to the EEA
Agreement,3
the procedure referred to at point 47(d) of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement,
the Act referred to at point 50 of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement:
Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1719 of 26 September 2016 establishing a
guideline on forward capacity allocation (‘FCA Regulation’),4 and in particular
Articles 4(6)(g) and 61 thereof, as adapted to the EEA Agreement,5
Whereas:
1 OJ L 1, 3.1.1994, p. 3.
2 OJ L 211, 14.8.2009, p. 1.
3 EEA Joint Committee Decision No. 93/2017 of 5 May 2017.
4 OJ L 259, 27.9.2016, p. 42.
5 EEA Joint Committee Decision No. 205/2020 of 11 December 2020.
Avenue des Arts 19H, 1000 Brussels, tel: +32 2 286 18 11, www.eftasurv.int

Page 2
1 INTRODUCTION
(1) The FCA Regulation lays down a range of requirements for the internal electricity
market, amongst them requirements for cross-zonal capacity allocation in the forward
markets. These requirements include requirements for a methodology for sharing
costs incurred to ensure firmness and remuneration of long-term transmission rights
(‘LTTRs’), developed by all transmission system operators (‘TSOs’) in accordance
with Article 61 of the FCA Regulation (‘FRC methodology’).
(2) Norway is part of the internal market for electricity through the EEA Agreement. The
objective of the EEA Agreement is to create a homogeneous European Economic
Area (‘EEA’). Relevant EU legislation for the internal electricity market is incorporated
into the EEA Agreement so that it applies throughout the whole EEA, ensuring
uniform application of laws relating to this market and thus ensuring a level playing
field throughout the EEA. Upon incorporation of the EU legislation into the EEA
Agreement, Norway has the obligation to implement the acquis covered by this
legislation.
(3) The FCA Regulation was incorporated into the EEA Agreement by Decision of the
EEA Joint Committee 205/2020, which entered into force on 31 July 2021.6 It is
therefore appropriate to apply the FCA Regulation’s requirements on the FRC
methodology to Norway.
(4) The present Decision follows from the request of the Norwegian regulatory authority
(‘RME’) that the EFTA Surveillance Authority (‘the Authority’) adopts a decision for
Norway on the proposal it received from the European Network of Transmission
System Operators for Electricity (‘ENTSO-E’) on behalf of the Norwegian TSO,
Statnett, for the FRC methodology for Norway (‘the Proposal’).
(5) The present Decision includes the following annex:
Annex I to Decision No. 058/26/COL of 18 March 2026 on the methodology
for sharing costs incurred to ensure firmness and remuneration of
long-term transmission rights (FRC) for Norway in accordance with
Article 61 of Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1719 establishing a
guideline on forward capacity allocation, determining the
methodology for the sharing of costs incurred to ensure firmness and
remuneration of long-term transmission rights for Norway, as
amended and approved by the EFTA Surveillance Authority
(Document No. 1586064).
For information purposes only, the Authority’s Decision is accompanied by a text
providing a track-changed version of the Proposal, reflecting the Authority’s
amendments (Document No. 1589529).
2 PROCEDURE
(6) On 20 October 2022, ENTSO-E on behalf of the Norwegian TSO, Statnett, submitted
the Proposal to RME.
(7) RME requested on 3 March 2023 (Document No. 1357183) the Authority to decide
on the Proposal in accordance with point 47 of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement, in
particular the adaptations set out in paragraph (d) thereof for the application of Article
8(1)(b) of the ACER Regulation, as incorporated into the EEA Agreement by Decision
of the EEA Joint Committee No. 93/2017, and Article 4(10) of the FCA, as
incorporated into the EEA Agreement by Decision of the EEA Joint Committee No.
205/2020 and having regard in particular to paragraph (9) of the preamble thereof.
6 The FCA Regulation was made binding in the internal legal order in Norway through national
measures, which entered into force on 1 August 2021.

Page 3
(8) On 3 July 2023, the Authority requested the European Union Agency for the
Cooperation of Energy Regulators (‘ACER’) to prepare a draft for the Authority’s
decision on the Proposal in accordance with point 47(d)(iv) of Annex IV to the EEA
Agreement (Document No. 1384919).
(9) On 12 December 2023, ACER provided the Authority with a draft for the Authority’s
decision on the Proposal, in accordance with point 47(d)(iv) of Annex IV to the EEA
Agreement (Documents No. 1594866, 1594865, 1594864, 1594863 and 1594862).
(10) On 10 February 2026, the Authority invited RME to express its views on the
Authority’s preliminary decision, in accordance with point 47(d)(iv) of Annex IV to the
EEA Agreement (Documents No. 1593395, 1593396, 1593397 and 1593398).
(11) On 26 February 2026, RME shared its views on the Authority’s preliminary decision
(Document No. 1592908). These views are summarised in section 5 of this Decision.
(12) This Decision is issued following the Authority’s review and amendment of the
Proposal, in cooperation with ACER. Annex I to this Decision sets out the FRC
methodology for Norway, pursuant to Articles 4(6)(g) and 61 of the FCA Regulation,
as amended and approved by the Authority.
(13) The Authority’s Decision determines the FRC methodology solely for Norway.
• For the purposes of this Decision, it is relevant to note that the FRC
methodology for the EU has been approved by ACER decisions, namely by
decisions of:
o 23 October 2020 (‘ACER Decision No. 25/2020’);
o 4 October 2021, replacing ACER Decision No. 25/2020 after an
appeal and remittal by ACER’s Board of Appeal (‘ACER Decision No.
12/2021’);7 and
o 22 March 2023, amending ACER Decision No. 12/2021 on the basis
of the proposal received from the EU TSOs (‘the EU Proposal’)
(‘ACER Decision No. 07/2023’).8
7 ACER Decision No. 12/2021 of 4 October 2021 on sharing costs incurred to ensure firmness and
remuneration of long-term transmission rights. ACER Decision No. 12/2021 is available under this
link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions/ACER%20Decisi
on%2012-2021%20on%20the%20FCA-FRC%20Methodology_0.pdf.
Annex I to Decision No. 12/2021 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions_annex/ACER%2
0Decision%20on%20the%20FCA-FRC%20Methodology%20-%20Annex%20I_0.pdf.
Annex Ia to Decision No. 12/2021 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions_annex/ACER%2
0Decision%20on%20the%20FCA-FRC%20Methodology%20-%20Annex%20Ia_0.pdf.
Annex II to Decision No. 12/2021 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions_annex/ACER%2
0Decision%20on%20the%20FCA-FRC%20Methodology%20-%20Annex%20II_0.pdf.
8 ACER Decision No. 07/2023 of 22 March 2023 is approving amendments to the methodology for
sharing costs incurred to ensure firmness and remuneration of long-term transmission rights. ACER
Decision No. 07/2023 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions/ACER_Decision
_07-2023_on_FRC.pdf.
Annex I to Decision No. 07/2023 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions_annex/ACER_D
ecision_07-2023_on_FRC_Annex_I.pdf.
Annex Ia to Decision No. 07/2023 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions_annex/ACER_D
ecision_07-2023_on_FRC_Annex_Ia.pdf.

Page 4
(14) ACER, when taking its decisions for the EU, held numerous dialogues and
exchanges with the relevant EEA TSOs and national regulatory authorities. The
dialogues and exchanges are documented in detail in ACER’s Decisions.
(15) The content of the Proposal, received by the Authority from RME, is aligned on the
EU Proposal’s content, upon which ACER decided for the EU by Decision No.
07/2023.
3 THE AUTHORITY’S COMPETENCE TO DECIDE ON THE PROPOSAL
(16) In accordance with point 47(d)(i) of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement, adapting Article
8 of the ACER Regulation, in cases involving one or more EFTA State(s), the
Authority is to adopt a decision addressed to the national regulatory authorities of the
concerned EFTA State(s), where ACER is competent on the EU side.
(17) On 3 March 2023, RME referred the Proposal to the Authority for a decision in
accordance with point 47(d)(i) of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement, adapting Article
8(1)(b) of the ACER Regulation.
(18) The Proposal’s content falls within the scope of Article 61 of the FCA Regulation. The
FCA Regulation was incorporated at point 50 of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement by
Decision of the EEA Joint Committee No. 205/2020, which entered into force on 31
July 2021.
(19) Following the incorporation of the FCA Regulation into the EEA Agreement, the FRC
methodology involves one EFTA State, namely Norway. In the EU, ACER decided
on the corresponding proposal through binding decisions.
(20) Consequently, it is the Authority’s competence to adopt a decision addressed to RME
to determine the FRC methodology for Norway.
4 SUMMARY OF THE PROPOSAL
(21) The Proposal includes the following elements:
(a) the ‘Whereas’ section and Title 1 containing Articles 1 and 2, which include the
subject matter and scope as well as definitions and interpretation;
(b) Title 2, covering the specification of the cost sharing remuneration, which
includes Articles 3, 4 and 5 that specify for eligible LTTRs among bidding zone
borders (‘BZBs’) of Long-Term net transmission capacity (‘NTC’) capacity
calculation regions (‘CCRs’), BZBs of Long-Term Flow-based CCRs and TSOs
on a BZB;
(c) Title 3, covering sharing of compensation costs, which includes Article 6 that
determines the cost sharing due to curtailment of LTTRs;
(d) Title 4, covering final provisions in Articles 7 and 8 on the publication,
implementation and revision of the FRC methodology and the language.
Annex II to Decision No. 07/2023 is available under this link:
https://www.acer.europa.eu/sites/default/files/documents/Individual%20Decisions_annex/ACER_D
ecision_07-2023_on_FRC%20_Annex_II.pdf.

Page 5
5 OBSERVATIONS RECEIVED BY THE AUTHORITY
(22) RME observed that the Authority’s preliminary decision supported the overall
efficiency of long-term capacity allocation within the EEA in line with Article 61 of the
FCA Regulation.
(23) RME further considered that the methodology as annexed to the Authority’s
preliminary decision supported the objectives of the FCA Regulation as outlined in
Article 3 of the FCA Regulation by providing clear, objective and non-discriminatory
rules for sharing the costs of firmness and remuneration of LTTRs. It considered that
the methodology contributed to effective long-term cross-zonal trade, efficient
capacity allocation and orderly price formation. RME also noted that the clear rules
enhanced transparency and in sum supported efficient market functioning and
system development.
(24) RME especially welcomed the adaptations made by the Authority to reflect the
relevant legal framework of the FCA Regulation and the Third Energy Package, as
incorporated into the EEA Agreement. RME considered that these adaptations made
were not only necessary, but appropriate and well-balanced against the need to
ensure a homogeneous approach, the latter being essential to ensure the functioning
of the internal market.
(25) RME found that the content of the preliminary decision fell within the scope of Article
61 of the FCA Regulation. Furthermore, RME found that the FCA Regulation and
Regulation (EC) No. 714/2009 of 13 July 2009 on conditions for access to the network
for cross-border exchanges in electricity and repealing Regulation (EC) No
1228/20039 provided the relevant framework for the determination of the FRC
methodology for Norway as set out in the Authority’s preliminary decision.
6 ASSESSMENT OF THE PROPOSAL
6.1. Legal framework
(26) Article 61 of the FCA Regulation sets out specific requirements for the FRC
methodology.
(27) In particular, according to Article 61(1) of the FCA Regulation, the cost of ensuring
firmness shall include costs incurred from compensation mechanisms associated
with ensuring firmness of cross-zonal capacities as well as the cost of re-dispatching,
countertrading and imbalance associated with compensating market participants and
be borne by TSOs. In addition, according to Article 61(3) of the FCA Regulation, the
FRC methodology shall be consistent with the methodology for congestion income
distribution (‘FCA-CID methodology’)10 under Article 57 of the same Regulation.
(28) Article 4(8) of the FCA Regulation requires proposals to include a proposed timescale
for its implementation and a description of the expected impact on the objectives of
the FCA Regulation.
(29) Article 30(7) of the FCA Regulation provides that Article 61 of the same Regulation
shall not apply to the TSOs of the bidding zone borders in the event that regulatory
9 OJ L 211, 14.8.2009, p. 15, as incorporated at point 20 of Annex IV to the EEA Agreement by
Decision of the EEA Joint Committee No. 93/2017 of 5 May 2017.
10 EFTA Surveillance Authority Decision No. 201/2025/COL of 3 December 2025 on the forward
capacity allocation congestion income distribution methodology for Norway. The Decision can be
found under this link:
https://www.eftasurv.int/cms/sites/default/files/documents/gopro/Case%2089429%20College%20D
ecision%20201%2025%20COL%20-%20Norway%20-
%20Decision%20on%20the%20congestion%20income%20distribution%20%28CID%29%20meth
odology.pdf.

Page 6
authorities decide that LTTRs shall not be issued by the respective TSOs or that other
long-term cross-zonal hedging products shall be made available by the respective
TSOs.
(30) As outlined in section 1 of this Decision, Article 1 of the EEA Agreement defines the
aim of the EEA Agreement as achieving a homogeneous EEA based on common
rules and equal conditions of competition.
(31) The Decisions of the EEA Joint Committee No. 93/2017 and No. 205/2020 set up a
mechanism catering for a homogeneous interpretation and application of the ACER
Regulation and the FCA Regulation throughout the EEA. Under these acts, the
Authority is the competent authority to issue binding decisions vis-à-vis the
concerned national regulatory authorities of the EFTA States in cases involving one
or more EFTA State(s), where ACER is the competent authority on the EU side. The
decisions of the Authority are to be based on drafts prepared by ACER. The Authority
is to invite the national regulatory authorities of the EFTA States to express their
views on the matter, taking full account of the urgency, complexity and potential
consequences of the matter.
6.2. Assessment of the legal requirements
(32) The Proposal provides the FRC methodology for Norway.
(33) Considering the objective of the EEA Agreement of achieving a homogeneous EEA
based on common rules, as emphasised in sections 1 and 6.1 of this Decision, the
Authority is to adopt decisions consistent with decisions taken by ACER on similar
matters.
(34) The essence of the FCA Regulation and the FRC methodology calls for a
homogeneous approach. This is needed to be able to reach the objective of
optimising the forward capacity allocation of the transmission system. The FRC
methodology will promote this objective, by laying down objective criteria and
solutions for the sharing of costs to be applied by all involved TSOs. The FCA
Regulation through the FRC methodology establishes rules on how LTTRs curtailed
before the day-ahead firmness deadline should be reimbursed or compensated by
TSOs to the holders of LTTRs. Having homogeneous rules throughout the EEA is
crucial for optimising the calculation and allocation of long-term cross-zonal capacity,
which is necessary for an efficient use of the interconnected grid and to ensure
fairness for market participants.
(35) The Authority, bearing in mind the need to ensure homogeneous rules across the
EEA, considers that the Proposal, to the extent it is aligned on the FCR methodology
set for the EU by ACER Decision No. 12/2021, complies with the above requirements.
(36) Furthermore, the Authority, in cooperation with ACER, assessed compliance of the
Proposal with the requirements incorporated into the EEA Agreement under the FCA
Regulation and as recalled above, and with due regard to the amendments made by
ACER to the equivalent proposal for the EU in its Decision No. 07/2023. The Authority
raises the following considerations in support of its amendment to the Proposal.
6.2.1.Legal assessment of the requirements in Article 61 of the FCA Regulation (Cost of
ensuring firmness and remuneration of long-term transmission rights)
(37) Article 61(1) of the FCA Regulation requires that the Proposal covers the cost of
ensuring firmness which includes costs incurred from compensation mechanisms
associated with ensuring firmness of cross-zonal capacities as well as the cost of re-
dispatching, countertrading and imbalance associated with compensating market
participants.

Page 7
(38) In its Articles 3, 4 and 5, the Proposal provides for the costs of remuneration of eligible
LTTRs attributed to the implicit daily allocation process based on flow-based capacity
calculation and coordinated net transmission capacity (‘cNTC’) calculation, to be
shared among and paid by the TSOs.
(39) Article 6 of the Proposal requires that the costs for ensuring firmness of LTTRs are
shared and paid by the TSOs. Article 1(6) of the Proposal further clarifies that
imbalance costs do not occur in case of curtailed LTTRs.
(40) Article 1(5) of the Proposal includes provisions on re-dispatching and countertrading
by referring to ‘costly remedial actions’ and the provisions set out in Article 74(1) of
the CACM Regulation.11
(41) Given the above, the Proposal complies with Article 61(1) of the FCA Regulation.
(42) Article 61(3) of the FCA Regulation requires that the Proposal is consistent with the
FCA-CID methodology,12 whereby the latter methodology needs to take into account
the CACM-CID methodology13 (as required by Article 57(2) of the FCA Regulation).
(43) Articles 3 and 4 of the Proposal provide for an approach as to how to consider the
costs of remuneration of eligible LTTRs attributed to the implicit daily allocation
process based on flow-based capacity calculation and cNTC calculation. The
proposed approach is to share costs per market time unit, as used in the FCA-CID
methodology and the CACM-CID methodology. Further, following the principles
described in Articles 3 and 4 of the Proposal, distribution of these costs should follow
the same proportional distributions as the distribution of congestion income in
accordance with the FCA-CID methodology and the CACM-CID methodology.
(44) For clarification and consistency with the CACM-CID methodology, the Authority has
replaced the reference to ‘external borders’ in Article 4(6) of the Proposal with ‘slack
hubs’ and added the related definition of ‘slack hubs’ in Article 2(1) of the Proposal.
The Authority has also added clarifications to Article 3, paragraphs (4) and (5), of the
Proposal, to ensure consistency with the corresponding provisions in paragraphs (3)
and (4) of Article 4 of the Proposal, which were proposed by the TSOs.
(45) Furthermore, consistency with the related CID methodologies is ensured by Article 7
of the Proposal which, in paragraphs (3) and (4), requires an amendment of the FRC
methodology in case of amendments to the CID methodologies.
(46) Therefore, the Proposal complies with Article 61(3) of the FCA Regulation.
6.2.2.Assessment of the requirements of Article 4(8) of the FCA Regulation
(47) The Proposal meets the requirement of Article 4(8) of the FCA Regulation concerning
the implementation timescale, because it contains, in Article 7(2) of the Proposal, a
timeline for its implementation.
11 OJ L 197, 25.7.2015, p. 24, as incorporated into the EEA Agreement by EEA Joint Committee
Decision No. 204/2020 of 11 December 2020.
12 EFTA Surveillance Authority Decision No. 201/2025/COL of 3 December 2025 on the forward
capacity allocation congestion income distribution methodology for Norway. The Decision can be
found under this link:
https://www.eftasurv.int/cms/sites/default/files/documents/gopro/Case%2089429%20College%20D
ecision%20201%2025%20COL%20-%20Norway%20-
%20Decision%20on%20the%20congestion%20income%20distribution%20%28CID%29%20meth
odology.pdf.
13 EFTA Surveillance Authority Decision No. 074/25/COL of 28 May 2025 on the congestion income
distribution methodology for Norway. The Decision can be found under this link:
https://www.eftasurv.int/cms/sites/default/files/documents/gopro/Case%2091961%20College%20D
ecision%20074%2025%20COL%20-%20Norway%20-
%20Decision%20on%20the%20congestion%20income%20distribution%20methodology.pdf.

Page 8
(48) Article 4(8) of the FCA Regulation also requires the Proposal for amendment to
include a description of its impact on the objectives of the FCA Regulation. The
Proposal complies with this requirement by assessing the Proposal against the
objectives of the FCA Regulation in recitals (11) to (16) of the ‘Whereas’-section. The
Authority has introduced a number of amendments to these recitals to clarify and
complete the assessment.
6.2.3.Assessment of other points of the Proposal
(49) The Authority found it necessary to add a new paragraph (2) in Article 1 of the
Proposal, which describes the scope of application of the FRC methodology. The
new recital (5) explains the categories of TSOs for which the non-application of the
FRC methodology is legally justified.
(50) In that regard, the Authority took into account the exchange reflected in ACER
Decision No. 07/2023 for the EU.
(51) The Authority clarified the consideration of costs from the returns of LTTRs in Article
4(3) of Annex I to this Decision, and, as a consequence, deleted the reference to
returned LTTRs in Article 4(2) of the Proposal. The Authority also added a
corresponding provision in Article 3(5) of the Proposal.
(52) The Authority further introduced some necessary editorial changes to improve the
readability and the structure of the Proposal.
(53) The Authority made changes in the ‘Whereas’-section, Article 1(2) and Article 2 of
the Proposal to reflect the application of this Decision for Norway, the applicable legal
framework and the adaptations foreseen for the incorporation of the relevant legal
framework into the EEA Agreement. The Authority also amended Article 7 of the
Proposal to reflect the procedure set out in point 47 of Annex IV to the EEA
Agreement for the determination of the FRC methodology for Norway.14
7 CONCLUSION
(54) For the above reasons, the Authority considers that the amendments detailed in
section 6 of this Decision are necessary to ensure that the Proposal is in line with the
requirements and the objectives of the FCA Regulation, as well as to improve the
editorial quality.
(55) Therefore, the Authority approves the Proposal subject to the necessary substantive
and editorial amendments. Annex I to this Decision sets out the FRC methodology
for Norway, as amended and approved by the Authority.
14 For the avoidance of doubt, deadlines set out in the Proposal, as amended by the Authority, that
precede the date of implementation of the FRC methodology annexed to the present Decision are
to be understood as references to the date of implementation of this methodology.

Page 9
HAS ADOPTED THIS DECISION:
1. This Decision applies to Norway.
2. The determination of the FRC methodology for Norway, pursuant to Article 61 of
the FCA Regulation, is approved as set out in Annex I to this Decision. Annex I
forms an integral part of this Decision.
3. This Decision is addressed to RME.
4. This Decision enters into force upon notification to RME.
5. This Decision is authentic in the English language.
Done at Brussels,
For the EFTA Surveillance Authority
For Arne Røksund Árni Páll Árnason Nuscha Wieczorek
President Responsible College Member College Member
For Melpo-Menie Joséphidès
Countersigning as Director,
Legal and Executive Affairs
